question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` call at line 172 — where user-defined `properties` map entries unconditionally override all defaults — to override `contracts.throttle.throttleByGas` from `false` to `true` or inject arbitrary consensus-node library keys, causing EVM execution behavior changes or denial of service for all users sharing the instance?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user, given that `executor.disableThrottles` is hardcoded to `true` in `buildTransactionProperties()`, craft a high-volume sequence of contract call requests that bypasses all gas and ops throttles, causing sustained CPU exhaustion and denial of service for all other users?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `contracts.throttle.throttleByOpsDuration` is hardcoded to `false` in `buildTransactionProperties()` to submit unlimited opcode-heavy contract calls without any per-operation throttling, causing unbounded CPU consumption and service degradation?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override at line 172 to inject a crafted value for `contracts.evm.version` that selects an older, known-vulnerable EVM version (e.g., `v0.30`) for all subsequent contract executions, bypassing security fixes introduced in later EVM versions?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override at line 172 to inject a crafted `contracts.chainId` value that mismatches the actual network chain ID, causing replay-attack acceptance of transactions signed for a different chain?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override at line 172 to set `contracts.systemContract.scheduleService.scheduleCall.enabled` to `false`, disabling the schedule service system contract for all users and causing a denial of service for schedule-dependent contract calls?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override at line 172 to inject a crafted `ledger.id` value that spoofs the ledger identity, causing the EVM to accept or produce results as if operating on a different Hedera network (e.g., MAINNET vs TESTNET)?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override at line 172 to set `nodes.nodeRewardsEnabled` to `false`, undermining the deliberate `Long.MAX_VALUE` guard on `nodes.minNodeRewardBalance` and causing unintended reward distribution to system accounts?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override at line 172 to set `nodes.minNodeRewardBalance` to `0`, removing the `Long.MAX_VALUE` guard that deliberately prevents reward payments to multiple system accounts, causing unintended reward distribution?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override at line 172 to set `tss.hintsEnabled` or `tss.historyEnabled` to `true`, enabling TSS subsystems that are deliberately disabled, potentially causing unexpected state transitions or resource exhaustion in the consensus node library?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override at line 172 to set `nodes.gossipFqdnRestricted` to `true`, causing the EVM execution layer to reject gossip connections and producing a denial of service for all contract call processing?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override at line 172 to inject a crafted `jumboTransactions.allowedHederaFunctionalities` value that removes `ContractCall` or adds unauthorized functionality types, causing contract calls to be rejected or unauthorized transaction types to be processed?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override at line 172 to inject a crafted `fees.simpleFeesEnabled` value of `true`, enabling simplified fee calculation that may undercharge or bypass fee enforcement for contract calls?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override at line 172 to inject a crafted `hedera.realm` or `hedera.shard` value, causing entity ID resolution to operate against a different shard/realm and producing cross-tenant data exposure or incorrect state lookups?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override at line 172 to inject a crafted `contracts.maxRefundPercentOfGasLimit` value exceeding 100, bypassing the `@Max(100)` constraint on `maxGasRefundPercentage` that only applies at the field level, causing over-refund of gas to callers?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override at line 172 to inject a crafted `contracts.sidecars` value enabling sidecar record generation, causing excessive memory and storage consumption for all contract calls and producing a denial of service?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override at line 172 to inject an arbitrarily large number of key-value pairs, causing the `transactionProperties` map to grow unboundedly and exhausting heap memory for all users sharing the JVM instance?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override at line 172 to inject a crafted `executor.disableThrottles` value of `false`, re-enabling throttles that are deliberately disabled, causing all contract calls to be throttled and producing a denial of service?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getSemanticEvmVersion()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a historical block call with a block number of `Long.MIN_VALUE` or a very large negative-equivalent long value passed to `getEvmVersionForBlock()`, causing `floorEntry()` to return `null` and falling back to the default `EVM_VERSION`, potentially bypassing version-specific security checks expected for that block range?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getSemanticEvmVersion()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a historical block call with a block number just below the first entry in `mainnetEvmVersionsMap()` (e.g., block `-1`) to force `getEvmVersionForBlock()` to return the default `EVM_VERSION` (v0.67) instead of `EVM_VERSION_0_30`, causing incorrect EVM semantics to be applied to historical state queries?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getSemanticEvmVersion()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a historical block call with a block number of `Long.MAX_VALUE` to force `getEvmVersionForBlock()` to return the latest EVM version for all historical queries, bypassing version-gated opcode restrictions that should apply to older blocks?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getSemanticEvmVersion()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `getSemanticEvmVersion()` calls `ContractCallContext.get()` without null-checking the returned context, causing a `NullPointerException` if the scoped value is unbound, leading to an unhandled exception and denial of service?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getSemanticEvmVersion()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a historical block call where `context.getRecordFile()` returns `null` (no matching record file for the requested block), causing a `NullPointerException` at `context.getRecordFile().getIndex()` in `getSemanticEvmVersion()` and producing an unhandled server error?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getEvmVersionForBlock(long blockNumber)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a historical block number that maps to `EVM_VERSION_0_30` via `getEvmVersionForBlock()`, forcing execution under an older EVM version with known opcode vulnerabilities (e.g., missing PUSH0 support or different gas costs), and exploit those version-specific behaviors to manipulate contract execution results?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getEvmVersionForBlock(long blockNumber)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a block number of exactly `44029066L - 1` on MAINNET to force `getEvmVersionForBlock()` to return `EVM_VERSION_0_30` instead of `EVM_VERSION_0_34`, exploiting the boundary condition in `floorEntry()` to select a version with different security properties?
